description: confirm_note_deletion passes note_id (a logical note identifier / file path string) directly to fs_trash_file_impl as the file path argument. REQ-FEED-019 specifies fs_trash_file receives a 'path' argument, but the confirm_note_deletion handler uses 'note_id' as the path. In feed.rs this works only if note_id IS the full file path. The +page.svelte FeedList passes note_id = file_path (from feed_initial_state) to visibleNoteIds, so in practice note_id === absolute path. However this coupling is never stated in the spec and is never tested in the integration test suite: tests/feed_handlers.rs does not test confirm_note_deletion at all. The verify-by-compilation claim in state.json (PROP-103) is not a behavioral test — it proves cargo check only, not that the correct path is passed. The TS adapter dispatchConfirmNoteDeletion sends { noteId, issuedAt } but the Rust handler calls fs_trash_file_impl(&note_id), treating the noteId as a file path. This hidden identity assumption (noteId === filePath) is an undocumented architectural decision with no test coverage and no spec rationale.

evidence.snippet: pub fn confirm_note_deletion(app: AppHandle, note_id: String, issued_at: String) -> Result<(), String> { ... match fs_trash_file_impl(&note_id) {
